description: Most relevant base data around the gross domestic product (GDP): the expenditure identity GDP = consumption + investment + government spending + net exports reproduced for the United States 2023, nominal GDP and population for the United States, Germany and Switzerland 2023 with the derived GDP per capita, and the year-over-year GDP growth derived by the system 'increase' formula through the time dimension (therefore not calc-validated). All GDP figures are nominal (current US dollars). Each word carries a Wikipedia ref plus the cleaned first sentence of the Wikipedia lead as the description so tooltips have text and the ref drives the refresh; the US expenditure components are set so the identity sums exactly to the stored GDP.

words.name: GDP
words.description: a monetary measure of the market value of all the final goods and services produced and rendered in a specific time period by a country.

words.name: GDP per capita
words.description: a country's gross domestic product divided by its population, used as a measure of average economic output per person.

words.name: population
words.description: the number of humans or other organisms living in a given region or area.

words.name: country
words.description: a distinct part of the world, such as a state, nation, or other political entity.

words.name: United States
words.description: a country primarily located in North America, comprising 50 states.

words.name: Germany
words.description: a country in Central Europe, between the Baltic and North seas and the Alps.

words.name: Switzerland
words.description: a landlocked country at the confluence of Western, Central and Southern Europe.

words.name: consumption
words.description: the use of goods and services by households to satisfy current needs and wants.

words.name: investment
words.description: the commitment of money or resources now in order to gain a future return or benefit.

words.name: spending
words.description: an outlay of money for goods or services

words.name: government
words.description: the system or group of people governing an organized community, generally a state.

words.name: export
words.description: a good or service produced in one country and sold into another country.

words.name: import
words.description: a good or service brought into one country from another.

words.name: growth
words.description: the increase in the inflation-adjusted value of the goods and services produced by an economy over time.

words.name: nominal
words.description: qualifier: measured at current prices, not adjusted for inflation

words.name: real
words.description: qualifier: adjusted for inflation to constant prices

words.name: year
words.description: the time taken by the Earth to complete one orbit around the Sun, about 365 days.

words.name: person
words.description: an individual human being; here the unit for counting people.

words.name: USD
words.description: the official currency of the United States and several other countries and territories.

words.name: percent
words.description: a number or ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

triples.name: government spending
triples.description: all spending by the government on consumption, investment, and transfer payments.

triples.name: net exports
triples.description: exports minus imports of goods and services, the trade-balance term of the GDP expenditure identity.

triples.name: nominal GDP
triples.description: gross domestic product measured at current market prices, not adjusted for inflation.

triples.name: real GDP
triples.description: gross domestic product adjusted for inflation and expressed in constant base-year prices.

triples.name: GDP growth
triples.description: the increase in a country's gross domestic product from one period to the next.

sources.name: BEA US GDP 2023
sources.description: US Bureau of Economic Analysis, gross domestic product and its expenditure components 2023 (current US dollars): consumption 18799.6, investment 4945.1, government 4562.6, exports 3051.1, imports 3996.4 (billions).

sources.name: IMF World Economic Outlook 2024
sources.description: International Monetary Fund, nominal GDP at current prices 2023 (current US dollars): Germany ~4456 bn, Switzerland ~885 bn.

sources.name: UN World Population Prospects 2024
sources.description: United Nations, mid-2023 population estimates: United States ~334.9 mn, Germany ~84.5 mn, Switzerland ~8.85 mn.

formulas.name: this
formulas.description: hardcoded formula to select now, today, this year, ...

formulas.name: next
formulas.description: hardcoded formula to select tomorrow, next week, next year, ...

formulas.name: prior
formulas.description: hardcoded formula to select yesterday, previous week, last year, ...
